examples.title: Algorithm: Basic Transaction Flow
examples.description: Fundamental steps for JDBC transaction management with commit and rollback
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ManageTransaction
BEGIN
 1. Set auto-commit = false
 2. Execute SQL statement 1 (e.g., UPDATE account SET balance = balance - X)
 3. Execute SQL statement 2 (e.g., UPDATE account SET balance = balance + X)
 4. IF all operations succeed THEN
 COMMIT transaction
 ELSE
 ROLLBACK transaction
 END IF
 5. Handle exceptions and cleanup resources
END
examples.explanation:
Line 1: Disables auto-commit to start transaction
Lines 2-3: Core database operations
Line 4: Atomic decision point for commit/rollback
Line 5: Essential for error handling and connection management
examples.timeComplexity: O(k) where k = number of SQL operations

examples.title: Algorithm: Savepoint Management
examples.description: Using savepoints for partial rollback in complex transactions
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SavepointTransaction
BEGIN
 1. Set auto-commit = false
 2. Execute initial operations (e.g., INSERT order header)
 3. CREATE SAVEPOINT order_created
 4. Execute dependent operations (e.g., INSERT order items)
 5. IF items insertion fails THEN
 ROLLBACK TO order_created
 LOG error
 ELSE
 COMMIT transaction
 END IF
END
examples.explanation:
Line 3: Creates recovery point after initial success
Line 5: Partial rollback preserves order header if items fail
Main transaction remains active after savepoint rollback
examples.timeComplexity: O(k + m) where k,m = operation counts before/after savepoint

examples.title: JDBC Bank Transfer Implementation
examples.description: Complete Java implementation for atomic money transfer
examples.explanation:
setAutoCommit(false): Begins transaction
PreparedStatements: Parameterized queries prevent SQL injection
try-with-resources: Automatically closes statements
commit(): Finalizes transaction on success
rollback(): Recovers from errors in catch block
finally: Resets auto-commit state
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per SQL operation + network roundtrips
